stars.description: A friendly library for working with PDFs
stars.highlights: Natural-pdf provides a user-friendly Python library for PDF processing with a focus on simplicity and accessibility. It offers intuitive functions for common PDF tasks like text extraction, page manipulation, and basic transformations without requiring deep PDF specification knowledge.
stars.worthReading: The library's clean API and practical examples make it approachable for developers who need to work with PDFs but want to avoid complex low-level libraries.

stars.description: A Rust chess library with the aim to be as feature-rich as possible
stars.highlights: A comprehensive Rust chess library focused on extensive feature support, including FEN, PGN, SAN, and UCI protocol handling. It aims to provide robust chess logic and move representation for developers building chess applications.
stars.worthReading: For Rust developers interested in game programming or chess applications, this library offers well-structured chess logic with multiple notation formats supported.

stars.description: open-arms-mini: cheap human like teleoperation device that supports human in the loop corrections
stars.highlights: Open-Arms-Mini is a low-cost teleoperation device designed for human-in-the-loop robotic corrections, enabling intuitive physical control of robots. It provides an accessible hardware solution for real-time human intervention in robotic tasks.
stars.worthReading: This project offers practical insights into affordable teleoperation hardware design, valuable for robotics researchers and hobbyists seeking hands-on control interfaces.
